mechanism_narrative: Positive retail sales momentum signals near-term consumption strength that lifts expected corporate revenues and supports S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the signal is the trailing growth rate of RSAFS.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 407805 as of two months before 2012-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A renewed rise in unemployment signals deterioration in household income expectations and recession risk, reducing expected earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 8.2 as of one month before 2012-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by non-negative mean 20-day S&P 500 returns and downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while CPI continues rising, realized real short rates fall, raising the present value of equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 and CPIAUCSL is above its level 6 months earlier
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 0.13 as of one month before 2012-04-15
fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 229.098 as of one month before 2012-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-FEDFUNDS rising-CPI signal has a negative average 60-day S&P 500 return or a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in industrial production signals weakening goods-sector output and operating leverage, reducing expected earnings revisions and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 96.2095 as of two months before 2012-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations or if the downside return differential is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts from depressed levels while unemployment remains high signals early-cycle private demand repair, improving confidence and cyclical earnings expectations, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% over 6 months while UNRATE remains above 7%
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
series:UNRATE
fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 698 as of two months before 2012-04-15
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 8.2 as of one month before 2012-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing rebounds during high unemployment do not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional returns, or if the triggered win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A material upward move in the federal funds rate signals tightening financial conditions and higher discount rates, lowering equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses upward by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 0.13 as of one month before 2012-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if upward FEDFUNDS crossings are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns and the negative-return hit rate is below 55% across walk-forward triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and a falling unemployment rate confirm broad labor-market improvement, raising expected household income and corporate sales, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is higher than 3 months earlier and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value was 132821 as of one month before 2012-04-15
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 8.2 as of one month before 2012-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up unemployment-down signal has non-positive mean 5-day S&P 500 returns or fails to beat the unconditional 5-day return by at least 5 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth captures broad demand expansion that supports aggregate earnings and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when measured as trailing two-quarter GDPC1 growth.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value was 13429.0 as of six months before 2012-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive two-quarter GDPC1 growth is not associated with positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if rolling 3-year directional accuracy is below 52%.
